data.text: I'll read the relevant files to understand what's needed before making changes.

data.output:
1	# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
2	# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
3	# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook
4	
5	# Configuration
6	colors: true
7	no_tty: false
8	assertion: false
9	
10	# Reusable command groups
11	command_groups:
12	  go_checks: &go_checks
13	    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
14	    tags: [go, backend]
15	
16	  security_checks: &security_checks
17	    tags: [security]
18	
19	  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
20	    tags: [format, style]
21	
22	# ====================
23	# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
24	# ====================
25	pre-commit:
26	  parallel: true
27	
28	  commands:
29	    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
30	    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
31	    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
32	    conflicts:
33	      name: "Check merge conflicts"
34	      run: |
35	        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
36	        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
37	        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
38	          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
39	          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
40	        done | head -1)
41	        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
42	          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
43	          exit 1
44	        fi
45	        exit 0
46	      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."
47	
48	    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
49	    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
50	    # debug-go:
51	    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
52	    #   glob: "*.go"
53	    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
54	    #   run: |
55	    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
56	    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
57	    #     else
58	    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
59	    #     fi
60	    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"
61	
62	    debug-python:
63	      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
64	      glob: "*.py"
65	      run: |
66	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
68	        else
69	          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
70	        fi
71	      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
72	
73	    # Go formatting - native gofmt
74	    go-fmt:
75	      <<: *go_checks
76	      name: "Go Format"
77	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
78	      run: |
79	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
80	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
81	          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
82	          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
83	          exit 1
84	        fi
85	      skip:
86	        - merge
87	        - rebase
88	
89	    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
90	    go-lint:
91	      <<: *go_checks
92	      name: "Go Lint"
93	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
94	      run: |
95	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
96	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
97	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
98	          exit 1
99	        fi
100	        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
101	        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
102	        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
103	        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
104	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
105	        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
106	        for pkg in $packages; do
107	          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
108	        done
109	      skip:
110	        - merge
111	        - rebase
112	
113	    # Go build - only for changed packages
114	    go-build:
115	      <<: *go_checks
116	      name: "Go Build"
117	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
118	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
119	      skip:
120	        - merge
121	        - rebase
122	
123	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
124	    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
125	    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
126	    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
127	    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
128	    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
129	    evidence-lint:
130	      <<: *go_checks
131	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
132	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
133	      run: |
134	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
135	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
136	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
137	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
138	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
139	        violations=""
140	        for f in $staged; do
141	          bare="${f#cli/}"
142	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
143	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
144	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
145	          fi
146	        done
147	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
148	          printf '%s' "$violations"
149	          exit 1
150	        fi
151	        exit 0
152	      skip:
153	        - merge
154	        - rebase
155	      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."
156	
157	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
158	    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
159	    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
160	    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
161	    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
162	    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
163	    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
164	    runtime-lint:
165	      <<: *go_checks
166	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
167	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
168	      run: |
169	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
170	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
171	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
172	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
173	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
174	        violations=""
175	        for f in $staged; do
176	          bare="${f#cli/}"
177	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
178	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
179	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
180	          fi
181	        done
182	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
183	          printf '%s' "$violations"
184	          exit 1
185	        fi
186	        exit 0
187	      skip:
188	        - merge
189	        - rebase
190	      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."
191	
192	    # Go tests - targeted testing
193	    go-test:
194	      <<: *go_checks
195	      name: "Go Tests"
196	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
197	      run: |
198	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
199	        for pkg in $packages; do
200	          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
201	            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
202	          fi
203	        done
204	      skip:
205	        - merge
206	        - rebase
207	      env:
208	        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"
209	
210	    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
211	    secrets:
212	      <<: *security_checks
213	      name: "Scan for secrets"
214	      run: |
215	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
216	          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
217	        else
218	          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
219	          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
220	          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')
221	
222	          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
223	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
224	            echo "Potential secrets detected"
225	            exit 1
226	          fi
227	
228	          # AWS keys
229	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
230	            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
231	            exit 1
232	          fi
233	
234	          # GitHub tokens
235	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
236	            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
237	            exit 1
238	          fi
239	        fi
240	      exclude:
241	        - "*.md"
242	        - "go.sum"
243	        - "package-lock.json"
244	        - "*_test.go"
245	        - "**/dist/**"
246	        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
247	      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"
248	
249	    # Binary and large file check
250	    large-files:
251	      name: "Check file sizes"
252	      run: |
253	        for file in {staged_files}; do
254	          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
255	          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
256	            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
257	            exit 1
258	          fi
259	        done
260	      exclude:
261	        - "*.png"
262	        - "*.jpg"
263	        - "*.jpeg"
264	        - "*.gif"
265	        - "*.svg"
266	        - "*.ico"
267	        - "*.pdf"
268	        - "*.woff"
269	        - "*.woff2"
270	        - "*.ttf"
271	        - "*.eot"
272	      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"
273	
274	    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
275	    ddx-validate:
276	      name: "Validate DDx"
277	      run: |
278	        # Only run if DDx files are staged
279	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
280	          exit 0
281	        fi
282	
283	        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
284	        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
285	        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
286	        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
287	        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
288	        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
289	        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
290	        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)
291	
292	        # Check for DDx command availability
293	        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
294	          ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
295	        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
296	          ./cli/ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
297	        fi
298	      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"
299	
300	    skill-schema:
301	      <<: *go_checks
302	      name: "Validate skill metadata"
303	      run: |
304	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
305	          exit 0
306	        fi
307	        make skill-schema
308	      skip:
309	        - merge
310	        - rebase
311	
312	    sync-embedded-skills:
313	      name: "Sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from top-level skills/ddx"
314	      root: "cli/"
315	      run: |
316	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^skills/ddx/'; then
317	          exit 0
318	        fi
319	        make copy-skills
320	        cd .. && git add cli/internal/skills/ddx/
321	      stage_fixed: true
322	      skip:
323	        - merge
324	        - rebase
325	      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from skills/ddx. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."
326	
327	    test-engineer-persona-drift:
328	      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
329	      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
330	      run: |
331	        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
332	          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
333	          exit 1
334	        fi
335	        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
336	          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
337	          exit 1
338	        fi
339	      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"
340	
341	# ====================
342	# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
343	# ====================
344	ci:
345	  parallel: true
346	
347	  commands:
348	    # Full conflict check
349	    conflicts-all:
350	      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
351	      run: |
352	        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
353	        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
354	          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
355	          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
356	          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
357	        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
358	          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
359	          exit 1
360	        fi
361	
362	    # Full secrets scan
363	    secrets-all:
364	      <<: *security_checks
365	      name: "Full secrets scan"
366	      run: |
367	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
368	          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
369	        else
370	          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
371	        fi
372	
373	    # Go full test suite
374	    go-test-all:
375	      <<: *go_checks
376	      name: "Full Go test suite"
377	      root: "cli/"
378	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
379	
380	    bead-schema:
381	      <<: *go_checks
382	      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
383	      run: 'make bead-schema'
384	
385	    skill-schema:
386	      <<: *go_checks
387	      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
388	      run: 'make skill-schema'
389	
390	    # Go formatting check all files
391	    go-fmt-all:
392	      <<: *go_checks
393	      name: "Check all Go formatting"
394	      root: "cli/"
395	      run: |
396	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
397	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
398	          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
399	          exit 1
400	        fi
401	
402	    # Go lint all files
403	    go-lint-all:
404	      <<: *go_checks
405	      name: "Lint all Go files"
406	      root: "cli/"
407	      run: |
408	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
409	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
410	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
411	          exit 1
412	        fi
413	        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m
414	
415	    # Go build all targets
416	    go-build-all:
417	      <<: *go_checks
418	      name: "Build all targets"
419	      root: "cli/"
420	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
421	
422	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
423	    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
424	    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
425	    evidence-lint-all:
426	      <<: *go_checks
427	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
428	      root: "cli/"
429	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'
430	
431	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
432	    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
433	    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
434	    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
435	    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
436	    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
437	    runtime-lint-all:
438	      <<: *go_checks
439	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
440	      root: "cli/"
441	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'
442	
443	    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
444	    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
445	    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
446	    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
447	    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
448	    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
449	    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
450	    # cli/ Go source.
451	    routing-lint-all:
452	      <<: *go_checks
453	      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
454	      root: "cli/"
455	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'
456	
457	    # YAML validation
458	    yaml-validate:
459	      name: "Validate YAML files"
460	      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
461	      run: |
462	        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
463	          for file in {all_files}; do
464	            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
465	          done
466	        fi
467	
468	# ====================
469	# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
470	# ====================
471	pre-push:
472	  parallel: false
473	
474	  commands:
475	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
476	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
477	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
478	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
479	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
480	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
481	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
482	    merge-policy:
483	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
484	      run: |
485	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
486	        remote="${1:-origin}"
487	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
488	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
489	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
490	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
491	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
492	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
493	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
494	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
495	          exit 0
496	        fi
497	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
498	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
499	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
500	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
501	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
502	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
503	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
504	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
505	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
506	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
507	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
508	          echo ""
509	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
510	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
511	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
512	          exit 1
513	        fi
514	        exit 0
515	      tags: [policy]
516	
517	    # Run full test suite before push
518	    full-tests:
519	      name: "Full test suite"
520	      root: "cli/"
521	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
522	      tags: [go, test]
523	
524	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
525	    no-debug:
526	      name: "No debug statements"
527	      run: |
528	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
529	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
530	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
531	          status=$?
532	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
533	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
534	            exit 0
535	          fi
536	          exit 1
537	        fi
538	
539	# Skip configuration
540	# Usage: LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test,secrets git commit
541	# Or: LEFTHOOK=0 git commit (skip all hooks)
542	
543	# Install hooks: lefthook install
544	# Run manually: lefthook run pre-commit
545	# Run CI checks: lefthook run ci
546

1	# Visual Suite — Principles, Tools, User Workflow
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Draft v6 (locked; reviewers' Q1–Q6 resolved)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	DDx ships a coordinated graphical suite covering three lenses on the product:
9	**principles**, **tools**, and **user workflow**. Each lens gets per-element
10	prompts plus a composite; the user-workflow lens is the capstone.
11	
12	Visual style is established via Google's **DESIGN.md** format spec
13	(github.com/google-labs-code/design.md) — markdown + YAML frontmatter
14	describing tokens (colors, typography, spacing, components) plus prose
15	rationale. Comes with a `@google/design.md` npm CLI for `lint`, `diff`,
16	`export` (Tailwind/DTCG), and `spec`. **Status: alpha, version `0.1.0`.**
17	
18	Project standardizes on **bun/bunx** for Node-ecosystem tooling, matching
19	existing precedent (the SvelteKit frontend at `cli/internal/server/frontend/`
20	already uses Bun per CLAUDE.md). `package.json` is the canonical declaration
21	surface for npm-delivered tools — no DDx wrapper, no FEAT-024 spec, no
22	DDx-owned lockfile.
23	
24	All visuals are generated artifacts (per the artifact-and-run-architecture
25	plan), live in the helix phase tree alongside the docs they depict, with
26	sidecar metadata and `generated_by` provenance.
27	
28	## Architecture
29	
30	```
31	DESIGN.md authored at repo root
32	      ↓
33	DESIGN.md → website palette via Hextra theme overrides (spike — see V3.5)
34	DESIGN.md tokens → image-generation prompts (palette anchors)
35	      ↓
36	Visual generation (one at a time, $10 cap, pressure-test gated)
37	      ↓
38	┌────────────────────────────────┐
39	│ Principle prompts (×6)         │
40	│   ↓                            │
41	│ Principle composite (capstone) │
42	├────────────────────────────────┤
43	│ Tool prompts (×4)              │
44	│   ↓                            │
45	│ Tool graphics + composite      │
46	├────────────────────────────────┤
47	│ User-workflow diagram (capstone)
48	└────────────────────────────────┘
49	      ↓
50	Website redesign (beads to be created — 28, 29) consumes
51	```
52	
53	## The three lenses
54	
55	### Lens 1 — Principles (×6 prompts; composite ships; standalone optional)
56	
57	One prompt per thesis principle. Lever/load/fulcrum metaphor is load-bearing.
58	
59	| Principle | Visual concept |
60	|---|---|
61	| 1. Abstraction is the lever | The lever arm — multi-level stack with intent transmitting |
62	| 2. Software is iteration over tracked work | Cyclic motion of the lever — discrete tracked items |
63	| 3. Methodology is plural | Multiple lever handles / interchangeable grips |
64	| 4. LLMs are stochastic, unreliable, costly | The load — non-uniform, probabilistic mass |
65	| 5. Evidence provides memory | Trail / chain of receipts left by the moving lever |
66	| 6. Human-AI collaboration is the fulcrum | The pivot — humans and AI in contact at the seam |
67	
68	All 6 prompts authored regardless of render decision. Composite ships first
69	(pressure-test the metaphor); standalone renders are optional follow-up if
70	the composite reads well alone.
71	
72	### Lens 2 — Tools (×4 + composite — full per-element + composite)
73	
74	| Tool | Visual concept |
75	|---|---|
76	| Bead tracker | DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked states |
77	| Document graph (artifacts) | Multi-node graph with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges |
78	| Agentic execution (run/try/work) | Three-layer wrapping; worktree isolation visible |
79	| Plugins | Modular composition; HELIX/Dun snapping into shared core |
80	
81	### Lens 3 — User workflow (capstone)
82	
83	Iterative diagram showing how a user actually works with DDx over time:
84	author/refine artifact → graph synthesizes context → bead created → agent
85	runs (`ddx try` / `ddx work`) → evidence captured → human re-aligns → loop
86	closes. Explanatory register; load-bearing artifact.
87	
88	## Storage — helix phase tree
89	
90	Visuals live alongside the docs they depict. DESIGN.md is the cross-cutting
91	exception at repo root.
92	
93	```
94	DESIGN.md                                          ← cross-cutting; repo root
95	package.json                                       ← repo root (bun-managed)
96	bun.lock                                           ← repo root (text lockfile)
97	docs/helix/00-discover/
98	├── product-vision.md
99	└── visuals/
100	    ├── principle-1-lever.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
101	    ├── ... (×6 principles)
102	    └── principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
103	docs/helix/01-frame/
104	├── prd.md
105	└── visuals/
106	    ├── tool-tracker.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
107	    ├── tool-doc-graph.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
108	    ├── tool-execution.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
109	    ├── tool-plugins.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
110	    ├── tools-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
111	    └── user-workflow.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
112	```
113	
114	Each visual artifact has `depends_on` pointing to the source doc and
115	`generated_by` pointing to its prompt source. First real test of
116	`generated_by` edges across the helix tree.
117	
118	## DESIGN.md is the visual style brief
119	
120	DESIGN.md *is* the style brief — its prose sections (Overview, Do's/Don'ts)
121	carry metaphor system and composition rules; its YAML carries tokens. No
122	separate STYLE.md needed.
123	
124	Two consumers:
125	- **Website (Hugo + Hextra):** spike palette injection via Hextra's
126	  `params.theme` / CSS variables (V3.5). Hextra ships precompiled CSS via
127	  Hugo Pipes; project-level Tailwind config consumption is unverified.
128	  Fall back to accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives prompts only) if
129	  Hextra reach is insufficient.
130	- **Image prompts:** prompts reference DESIGN.md palette by hex and named
131	  tokens for cross-prompt consistency anchor.
132	
133	Repo placement: `/DESIGN.md` (brand-level artifact for the whole product).
134	
135	## Tool dependency declaration: `package.json` (no DDx wrapper)
136	
137	Standard Node convention. No FEAT-024 spec, no DDx-owned lockfile, no
138	`ddx tool run` wrapper.
139	
140	```bash
141	bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0      # adds to package.json + bun.lock
142	bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md      # standard invocation
143	```
144	
145	`ddx doctor` extension scans known `package.json` locations (root,
146	`website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) and reports `bun install` if
147	`node_modules/` is missing or out of date. `ddx init` summary mentions
148	discovered `package.json` files.
149	
150	DDx never wraps `bunx`; users invoke npm-ecosystem tools directly via
151	standard `bunx <pkg>` commands.
152	
153	## Generation strategy
154	
155	- **Generator:** `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` (Gemini 3 Pro Image preview)
156	  by default.
157	- **Each visual is a `generate-artifact` run** per the artifact-and-run plan.
158	- **`SYSTEM_TEMPLATE` override** for sober/utilitarian style (default
159	  pulls toward "dreamy illustration").
160	- **DESIGN.md tokens injected** into every prompt for palette/typography
161	  consistency.
162	- **Sequential rendering with $10 total budget cap and $1.50/image halt
163	  trigger.** Render one visual at a time; evaluate against acceptance
164	  criteria before generating the next. Halt on either cap. Per-image
165	  cap prevents one runaway prompt eating the budget.
166	- **First real dogfood** of the artifact infrastructure (sidecar I/O,
167	  `generated_by` edges, `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI). Bugs surfaced here
168	  feed back into beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007),
169	  and the artifact-regenerate implementation.
170	- **Regen policy:** every visual `depends_on` DESIGN.md and its prompt
171	  source. Either-source change marks the visual stale via the separate
172	  `generated_by` staleness rule. Regen is explicit (`ddx artifact regenerate`),
173	  not automatic — operator decides when to spend tokens.
174	
175	## Acceptance criteria per visual
176	
177	- Renders cleanly at intended placement (homepage hero, docs page, README)
178	- Contrast OK in light and dark mode
179	- Mobile crop preserves the message
180	- Alt text drafted and in sidecar
181	- File size budget under 200KB (compress / downsize as needed)
182	- Metaphor parses without explanatory copy
183	- **Brand-fit: sober/utilitarian register matching DDx's docs surface; no
184	  AI-gloss, no kitsch, no dreamy illustration**
185	
186	## Risks
187	
188	- **DESIGN.md is alpha** (`0.1.0`). Pin exact `0.1.0`; budget for both
189	  format spec AND `--format tailwind` exporter breaking on bumps (two
190	  alpha surfaces stack).
191	- **Hextra Tailwind path unverified.** V3.5 is a spike. Expected outcome:
192	  palette override via Hextra theme params works; if not, fall back to
193	  accept-divergence rather than fork Hextra.
194	- **Cross-prompt style consistency** on `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` is
195	  unproven. DESIGN.md tokens anchor palette but composition consistency
196	  across 6+ subsections is genuine risk. Composite-first sequencing
197	  (V6 before V7) limits exposure.
198	- **Metaphor strain when rendered.** Pressure-test in V6 before committing
199	  further. If lever/fulcrum/load doesn't carry, revise the visual concept
200	  (not principle wording).
201	- **Artifact-infra bugs** could block visual generation. Plan accepts
202	  iteration: bugs → fixes in artifact-and-run beads → resume.
203	- **Cost overrun.** $10 cap is for the full run; halt at cap and reassess.
204	  Sequential generation gives visibility; cache-on-prompt-hash prevents
205	  accidental re-spending.
206	- **Two alpha surfaces.** DESIGN.md format and exporter both alpha.
207	  Pinning `0.1.0` exactly mitigates but doesn't eliminate risk; budget
208	  ~half a day per bump for migration.
209	
210	## Bead breakdown
211	
212	### Tooling foundation (gates the rest)
213	
214	- **V2.** Extend `ddx doctor` (and optionally `ddx init` summary) to detect
215	  `package.json` at known locations (root, `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`)
216	  and report `bun install` if `node_modules/` is missing or stale. ~100 LoC.
217	
218	### DESIGN.md integration
219	
220	- **V3.** Author `DESIGN.md` at repo root. Capture existing precedent
221	  (logo, current rgba triad, terminal-demo tone). Tokens (colors,
222	  typography, spacing, components); prose Overview, Do's/Don'ts, metaphor
223	  system. Run `bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0` at repo root; lint
224	  with `bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md`. **Treat as foundation,
225	  not throwaway spike** — DDx has no visual language today; embedding
226	  DESIGN.md is correct. Adapt as the format evolves.
227	- **V3.5.** Wire DESIGN.md → website (Hugo + Hextra) palette. Try Hextra
228	  `params.theme` / CSS-variable override path first. Fall back to
229	  accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives image prompts only) if Hextra
230	  reach insufficient. Document outcome in bead resolution.
231	- **V3.6.** Wire DESIGN.md → frontend (SvelteKit + Tailwind v4) at
232	  `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js`. Use
233	  `bunx @google/design.md export --format tailwind` to produce a
234	  theme JSON the frontend's tailwind.config.js consumes. Easier path
235	  than Hextra (frontend already uses Tailwind); ensures DESIGN.md ROI
236	  even if V3.5 falls back to accept-divergence. **Goal: align on a new
237	  visual language across the entire product surface.**
238	- **V4.** Lefthook + CI lint integration: `bunx @google/design.md lint
239	  DESIGN.md` runs on commit if DESIGN.md is modified.
240	
241	### Principles lens
242	
243	- **V5.** Author all 6 principle-graphic prompts at
244	  `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principle-N-*.prompt.md`.
245	- **V6.** Author principle-composite prompt + generate **(first render —
246	  pressure-test gate; budget tracking starts here)**. If the lever/fulcrum/
247	  load metaphor doesn't render usefully, pause and revise before proceeding.
248	  **Hard-blocked on artifact infrastructure**: beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010
249	  three-layer substrate), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by),
250	  and `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI implementation must be merged first.
251	  No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips.
252	- **V7** *(optional, conditional on V6 outcome).* Render the 6 principle
253	  subsections standalone. Skip if composite suffices or if cross-prompt
254	  consistency fails.
255	
256	### Tools lens
257	
258	- **V8.** Author 4 tool-graphic prompts at `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`.
259	- **V9.** Generate 4 tool graphics + tool composite **(sequential, budget-tracked)**.
260	
261	### Workflow capstone
262	
263	- **V10.** Author user-workflow prompt + generate **(sequential,
264	  budget-tracked)**. Explanatory register; load-bearing.
265	
266	### Website integration (downstream — beads not yet created)
267	
268	- Replace 6 feature cards on landing with 6 principle cards.
269	- Add Why DDx top-nav page (exposition around principles).
270	- Concept "Why this exists" callouts.
271	- Replace README tagline with thesis.
272	- E2E test updates.
273	- Visual integration consumes V6/V9/V10 outputs.
274	
275	## Reviewer ask
276	
277	1. **Tool-declaration simplification** — `package.json` + `ddx doctor`
278	   detection only, no FEAT-024, no DDx wrapper. Right reduction, or did we
279	   lose something load-bearing?
280	2. **bun standardization** — repo already uses Bun for the frontend per
281	   CLAUDE.md. Any subdirectory or build step that breaks if root tooling
282	   moves to bun (e.g., CI assumed npm)?
283	3. **Multiple `package.json` scan in `ddx doctor`** — known set (root,
284	   `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) vs glob discovery? Static
285	   list is simpler; glob risks scanning user output dirs.
286	4. **`package.json` placement for `@google/design.md`** — root chosen for
287	   cross-cutting linter. Concerns about polluting repo root with a
288	   `package.json`/`node_modules` when most of the project is Go?
289	5. **Hextra spike fallback (accept-divergence)** — if Hextra can't consume
290	   DESIGN.md tokens, website CSS stays Hextra-default and DESIGN.md drives
291	   image prompts only. Is image-prompt-only consumption sufficient ROI to
292	   justify the DESIGN.md effort?
293	6. **Cost cap $10 sequential** — realistic for 11+ Gemini-3-Pro-Image
294	   generations at 2K? Should we set a per-image cost ceiling too?
295	7. **Acceptance criteria** — anything missing? Brand-fit / kitsch judgment?
296	8. **Alpha-stack risk** — two alpha surfaces (DESIGN.md format + exporter).
297	   Sufficient to pin `0.1.0`, or should we wait until v1?
298	9. **Helix-tree storage of visuals** — `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/`
299	   and `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`. Anti-patterns or convention violations?
300	10. **Anything missing.**
301	
302	Codebase context:
303	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md`
304	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/CLAUDE.md` (bun precedent)
305	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/website/` (Hugo + Hextra; no Tailwind today)
306	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/server/frontend/` (existing bun/SvelteKit setup)
307	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`
308	- DESIGN.md spec: github.com/google-labs-code/design.md
309	
310	Respond with sections (omit empty):
311	**Keep:** correct, load-bearing
312	**Cut:** remove
313	**Missing:** gaps
314	**Risks:** verification needed (cite file:line)
315	**Questions:** require USER input
316	
317	Concise. 2 sentences per finding. Severity-ordered.
318

data.text: Now let me check the CI workflow and if DESIGN.md exists at the repo root.

data.output:
1	name: CI
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [ main, develop ]
6	  pull_request:
7	    branches: [ main ]
8	
9	# Minimal permissions by default
10	permissions:
11	  contents: read
12	  checks: write
13	  pull-requests: write
14	
15	jobs:
16	  # Main CI job using lefthook
17	  ci:
18	    name: CI Validation
19	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
20	    steps:
21	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
22	      with:
23	        fetch-depth: 0  # Need full history for some checks
24	    
25	    - name: Set up Go
26	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
27	      with:
28	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
29	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
30	
31	    - name: golangci-lint
32	      uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v8
33	      with:
34	        version: v2.11.4
35	        working-directory: cli
36	        skip-cache: false
37	
38	    - name: Install lefthook
39	      run: |
40	        # Install using Go for better reliability in CI
41	        go install github.com/evilmartians/lefthook@latest
42	        echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
43	
44	    - name: Verify git-subtree availability
45	      run: |
46	        # git-subtree should be included with git, verify it's available
47	        if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48	          echo "git-subtree not found, attempting to install..."
49	          # On some distros it's a separate package
50	          sudo apt-get update
51	          sudo apt-get install -y git-subtree || true
52	          # Verify again
53	          if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54	            echo "ERROR: git-subtree is not available"
55	            exit 1
56	          fi
57	        fi
58	        echo "✓ git-subtree is available"
59	
60	    - name: Set up Bun
61	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
62	      with:
63	        bun-version: latest
64	
65	    - name: Build CLI binary
66	      run: |
67	        cd cli
68	        make build
69	
70	    - name: Validate bead schema
71	      run: make bead-schema
72	
73	    - name: Validate skill metadata
74	      run: make skill-schema
75	
76	    - name: Validate ddx skill (agentskills.io conformance)
77	      run: scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate
78	
79	    - name: Run CI validation
80	      run: lefthook run ci
81	      env:
82	        LEFTHOOK_QUIET: meta,execution
83	
84	    - name: Guard server state against test pollution
85	      run: |
86	        set -euo pipefail
87	
88	        export XDG_DATA_HOME="$(mktemp -d)"
89	
90	        cd cli
91	        go test ./... -count=1
92	
93	        ./build/ddx server --addr 127.0.0.1 --port 17743 --tsnet=false > ../ddx-state-guard-server.log 2>&1 &
94	        server_pid=$!
95	        cleanup() {
96	          kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
97	          wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
98	        }
99	        trap cleanup EXIT
100	
101	        state_file="$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json"
102	        for _ in $(seq 1 20); do
103	          if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
104	            break
105	          fi
106	          sleep 0.25
107	        done
108	
109	        if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
110	          cat ../ddx-state-guard-server.log
111	          echo "server-state.json was not created"
112	          exit 1
113	        fi
114	
115	        project_count="$(jq '.projects | length' "$state_file")"
116	        if [ "$project_count" -gt 20 ]; then
117	          jq '.projects[] | {name, path}' "$state_file"
118	          echo "server-state.json contains $project_count projects; expected <= 20"
119	          exit 1
120	        fi
121	
122	        if jq -e '.projects[]? | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)"))' "$state_file" >/dev/null; then
123	          jq '.projects[] | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)")) | {name, path}' "$state_file"
124	          echo "server-state.json contains Go test temp-dir project entries"
125	          exit 1
126	        fi
127	
128	  # Separate build job for matrix builds
129	  build:
130	    name: Build
131	    needs: ci
132	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
133	    strategy:
134	      matrix:
135	        os: [linux, darwin, windows]
136	        arch: [amd64, arm64]
137	        exclude:
138	          - os: windows
139	            arch: arm64
140	    defaults:
141	      run:
142	        working-directory: cli
143	    steps:
144	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
145	    
146	    - name: Set up Go
147	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
148	      with:
149	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
150	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
151	    
152	    - name: Build binary
153	      run: |
154	        GOOS=${{ matrix.os }} GOARCH=${{ matrix.arch }} \
155	          go build -v -o ../bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }} .
156	    
157	    - name: Upload artifacts
158	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
159	      with:
160	        name: ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
161	        path: bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
162	        retention-days: 7
163	
164	  # Integration tests
165	  integration:
166	    name: Integration Tests
167	    needs: ci
168	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
169	    defaults:
170	      run:
171	        working-directory: cli
172	    steps:
173	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
174	
175	    - name: Set up Go
176	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
177	      with:
178	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
179	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
180	
181	    - name: Build binary
182	      run: go build -o ../bin/ddx .
183	
184	    - name: Run E2E smoke tests
185	      run: go test -v ./cmd -run TestE2ESmokeJourney -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../e2e-test-output.txt
186	
187	    - name: Upload E2E test output
188	      if: always()
189	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
190	      with:
191	        name: e2e-test-output
192	        path: e2e-test-output.txt
193	        retention-days: 7
194	
195	  frontend-e2e:
196	    name: Frontend Playwright E2E
197	    needs: ci
198	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
199	    steps:
200	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
201	
202	    - name: Set up Go
203	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
204	      with:
205	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
206	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
207	
208	    - name: Set up Bun
209	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
210	      with:
211	        bun-version: latest
212	
213	    - name: Install frontend dependencies
214	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
215	      run: bun install
216	
217	    - name: Build frontend bundle
218	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
219	      run: bun run build
220	
221	    - name: Build CLI binary
222	      working-directory: cli
223	      run: make build
224	
225	    - name: Install Playwright browsers
226	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
227	      run: bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium
228	
229	    - name: Run frontend unit tests
230	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
231	      run: bun run test
232	
233	    - name: Run frontend Playwright E2E tests
234	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
235	      run: bun run test:e2e

data.text: Let me check the root package.json to confirm `@google/design.md` is already a dependency.
`@google/design.md@0.1.0` is already in devDependencies. Now I'll add the two pieces: lefthook pre-commit hook and CI step.

input.new_string:
design-md-lint:
      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
      glob: "DESIGN.md"
      run: |
        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
          exit 0
        fi
        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
        bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md

data.text:
Committed. The two changes are in place:

1. **`lefthook.yml`** — `design-md-lint` command in `pre-commit`, scoped via `glob: "DESIGN.md"`, runs `bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md` only when DESIGN.md is staged. Includes a graceful skip if `bun` isn't available.

2. **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — Two new steps in the `ci` job after the existing Bun setup: `bun install --frozen-lockfile` (installs root `node_modules` including `@google/design.md@0.1.0`), then `bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md`.
